criteria.text: EventLog Analyzer Windows service is running with a dedicated low-privilege service account
criteria.recommendation: Use a dedicated service account instead of LocalSystem or Administrator. The service account needs minimal rights: Log on as a service, Event Log Readers group membership on source machines, and read access to syslog folders. Avoid using Domain Admin.

criteria.text: EventLog Analyzer is on the latest available build (13000 series)
criteria.recommendation: Update to the latest 13000-series build. This series includes: AI-assisted log parser, enhanced correlation engine with MITRE ATT&CK rules, expanded cloud source support (Azure, AWS, GCP, Salesforce, Google Workspace), and improved threat intelligence feeds. Apply via Admin > Product Updates.

criteria.text: Server hardware meets requirements for log volume and source count
criteria.recommendation: Validate hardware against ManageEngine sizing guide for ELA. For environments with >500 devices/10,000 EPS: 32GB RAM, 8 CPU cores, dedicated SSD array. Storage: calculate 1GB per device per day as baseline. Under-provisioning leads to log drop and alerting failures.

criteria.text: External database (MSSQL or PostgreSQL) is used for production deployments
criteria.recommendation: Migrate from bundled Elasticsearch/PostgreSQL to external MSSQL or PostgreSQL for production. External databases provide better query performance, HA support, and enterprise backup integration. This is mandatory for deployments with >1000 devices or >1TB log data.

criteria.text: High Availability is configured for business continuity
criteria.recommendation: Configure EventLog Analyzer HA to prevent single points of failure in your SIEM collection infrastructure. HA ensures log collection continues during failover. Configure a standby node and test failover procedures quarterly.

criteria.text: Log storage capacity planning is in place with retention policies defined
criteria.recommendation: Define and enforce log retention policies based on compliance requirements: PCI-DSS requires 1 year online + 1 year offline, HIPAA requires 6 years, SOX requires 7 years. Configure tiered storage: online (hot), near-line (warm), archive (cold). Navigate to Settings > Log Retention Policy.

criteria.text: All Windows hosts (servers, DCs, workstations) are sending logs via WMI or agent
criteria.recommendation: Deploy EventLog Analyzer agents on all Windows hosts for reliable, real-time log collection. Agent-based collection is more reliable than WMI for high-volume environments. Prioritize: Domain Controllers, member servers, then workstations. Verify all expected hosts are in 'Active' status under Hosts.

criteria.text: Syslog sources (network devices, Linux, firewalls) are configured with correct facility/severity
criteria.recommendation: Configure all network devices (routers, switches, firewalls, proxies) to send syslog to EventLog Analyzer. Ensure correct port (UDP 514 / TCP 514 / TCP 1468 for encrypted syslog). Enable facility-based separation for cleaner log management. Verify receipt in Hosts > Syslog.

criteria.text: Cloud platform logs are configured (AWS CloudTrail, Azure Monitor, GCP Audit Logs)
criteria.recommendation: Configure cloud log sources for all cloud platforms in use: AWS CloudTrail (via S3 or direct API), Azure Monitor/Activity Logs (via Event Hub), GCP Audit Logs (via Pub/Sub), and cloud-native services (Lambda, Azure Functions, GCP Cloud Run). This is critical for hybrid cloud threat visibility.

criteria.text: SaaS application logs are collected (Salesforce, Office 365, Google Workspace, Box, Dropbox)
criteria.recommendation: Connect SaaS audit log sources to EventLog Analyzer for comprehensive identity and data activity monitoring. Configure: Microsoft 365 Unified Audit Log (via Management API), Google Workspace (via Admin SDK), Salesforce (via EventLogFile API), Box, and Dropbox. Navigate to Log Sources > SaaS Applications.

criteria.text: Network device logs (firewalls, routers, switches, proxies, VPN) are fully collected
criteria.recommendation: Ensure all network devices forward logs: Cisco ASA/Firepower, Palo Alto, Fortinet, Check Point, Juniper, Zscaler proxy, VPN concentrators. Network device logs provide perimeter visibility essential for detecting C2 traffic, lateral movement, and data exfiltration. Add via Hosts > Add Network Device.

criteria.text: Database server logs (MSSQL, Oracle, MySQL) are collected
criteria.recommendation: Configure database audit log collection for MSSQL, Oracle, and MySQL. Database logs detect SQL injection patterns, privilege abuse, bulk data extraction, and unauthorized schema changes. Enable SQL Server audit and configure syslog output, or use the EventLog Analyzer JDBC connector.

criteria.text: Application logs (IIS, Apache, custom apps) are parsed and collected
criteria.recommendation: Add critical application logs for web servers (IIS, Apache, Nginx) and custom applications. Web server logs detect injection attacks, brute force, directory traversal, and web shell activity. Configure W3C format parsing for IIS and Common/Combined log format for Apache/Nginx.

criteria.text: Identity provider logs (Active Directory, LDAP, RADIUS, ADFS) are collected
criteria.recommendation: Collect all identity provider logs as the primary data source for detecting authentication attacks. Required sources: Windows Security Event Log from all DCs (Events 4624, 4625, 4648, 4720, 4726, 4728, 4756), ADFS logs, NPS/RADIUS, and certificate services.

criteria.text: Pre-built log parsers are verified and working correctly for all log sources
criteria.recommendation: Verify that pre-built parsers correctly parse all log sources. Check for 'Unknown' or 'Unparsed' log entries in Log Sources > Parsing Status. Unparsed logs cannot be searched, correlated, or alerted on. Navigate to Settings > Log Parser to review parser health.

criteria.text: Custom log parsers are created for proprietary/unsupported log formats
criteria.recommendation: Create custom parsers for any custom application or proprietary device logs that don't match pre-built parsers. Use the AI-assisted log parser (new in 13000 series) to automatically suggest regex patterns from sample logs. Navigate to Settings > Custom Log Parser > AI Parser Assist.

criteria.text: Log enrichment (IP geolocation, hostname resolution, user context) is configured
criteria.recommendation: Enable log enrichment to add context to raw events: IP geolocation (country, ASN), DNS hostname resolution, user department/role from AD, threat intelligence lookups. Enriched logs dramatically improve analyst efficiency during incident investigation. Configure under Settings > Log Enrichment.

criteria.text: Pre-built correlation rules are enabled for common attack patterns
criteria.recommendation: Enable all relevant pre-built correlation rules. Prioritize: Brute Force Detection, Account Compromise, Lateral Movement, Data Exfiltration, Malware Activity. Review and tune thresholds to reduce false positives. Navigate to Correlation > Manage Rules and enable rules for your environment.

criteria.text: Custom correlation rules are created for organization-specific threat scenarios
criteria.recommendation: Create custom correlation rules for your environment-specific threats: privileged account used from VPN + unusual time of day, service account authenticating interactively, new domain admin account followed by logon outside business hours. Document each rule's rationale.

criteria.text: MITRE ATT&CK aligned detection rules are enabled and mapped
criteria.recommendation: Enable MITRE ATT&CK mapped detection rules in the 13000-series correlation engine. These rules provide coverage across MITRE tactics: Initial Access, Execution, Persistence, Privilege Escalation, Defense Evasion, Credential Access, Discovery, Lateral Movement, Collection, Exfiltration, Impact. Track coverage using the ATT&CK Navigator integration.

criteria.text: Correlation rule performance is monitored and rules are tuned to reduce false positives
criteria.recommendation: Review correlation rule performance monthly. High false-positive rules cause alert fatigue—analysts begin ignoring all alerts. Use the correlation rule performance report to identify high-noise rules and tune thresholds. Target <100 correlation alerts per day per analyst.

criteria.text: Threat Intelligence feeds (STIX/TAXII, MISP, built-in ManageEngine feeds) are configured
criteria.recommendation: Configure threat intelligence feeds to automatically identify malicious IPs, domains, URLs, and file hashes in log data. Enable built-in ManageEngine threat feeds, and optionally add commercial feeds (VirusTotal, Recorded Future, Anomali) or open feeds (AlienVault OTX, Abuse.ch). Navigate to Threat Intelligence > Feed Management.

criteria.text: IOC (Indicators of Compromise) database is regularly updated (at minimum daily)
criteria.recommendation: Configure automatic daily updates for all threat intelligence feeds. Stale IOC databases miss new threats. Enable auto-update in Threat Intelligence > Feed Settings > Update Schedule. Verify last update timestamp daily via the TI dashboard.

criteria.text: Threat Intelligence is integrated with alert correlation (TI-enriched correlation alerts)
criteria.recommendation: Enable TI enrichment in correlation rules so that any log matching a known malicious IOC generates an immediate high-priority alert. This dramatically improves time-to-detection for known threat actors. Configure under Threat Intelligence > Alert Configuration.

criteria.text: Compliance reports are configured and scheduled (PCI-DSS, HIPAA, SOX, FISMA, GDPR, NIST, CCPA)
criteria.recommendation: Configure automated compliance report delivery for all applicable frameworks. EventLog Analyzer includes pre-built reports for: PCI-DSS, HIPAA, SOX, FISMA, GLBA, NIST SP 800-53, ISO 27001, GDPR, and CCPA. Schedule quarterly compliance audits and archive reports for regulators. Navigate to Reports > Compliance.

criteria.text: MITRE ATT&CK reports are enabled and reviewed for coverage gaps
criteria.recommendation: Use MITRE ATT&CK reports to identify coverage gaps in your detection capability. Map which ATT&CK techniques you have telemetry for vs. which are blind spots. Navigate to Reports > Threat Reports > MITRE ATT&CK Heatmap. Use gap analysis to prioritize new log source onboarding.

criteria.text: Custom reports are created for management and executive reporting
criteria.recommendation: Create executive summary reports showing: monthly security incidents, top attacked systems, authentication failure trends, compliance posture score. Schedule delivery to management monthly. Navigate to Reports > Custom Reports > Report Builder.

criteria.text: Log search and forensic investigation capabilities are tested and SOC team is trained
criteria.recommendation: Ensure SOC analysts are proficient with EventLog Analyzer's log search (universal search, field-level search, boolean operators). Conduct quarterly training exercises: simulate an incident and task analysts with reconstructing the attack using only log search. Test query response times.

criteria.text: Critical alert profiles are configured for high-priority security events
criteria.recommendation: Configure alert profiles for: Windows account lockouts, admin account changes, policy violations, malware detection events, firewall policy changes, authentication to privileged systems. Set escalation thresholds. Navigate to Alerts > Manage Alert Profiles.

criteria.text: Alert escalation workflows are defined with on-call rotation support
criteria.recommendation: Define multi-tier escalation: Level 1 (email to SOC analyst), Level 2 (SMS/PagerDuty after 15 min), Level 3 (call CISO after 30 min). Integrate with ticketing systems (ServiceDesk Plus, Jira, ServiceNow) for automatic incident ticket creation on high-severity alerts.

criteria.text: Alert noise is managed — false positive rate is below 10%
criteria.recommendation: Review alert effectiveness quarterly. Target: <10% false positive rate. Tune thresholds, add exclusions for known-good baselines (backup jobs, vulnerability scanners), and suppress duplicate alerts. Enable suppress-duplicates for correlation rules that fire repeatedly within short windows.

criteria.text: HTTPS / TLS is enforced for web console with valid SSL certificate
criteria.recommendation: Configure TLS 1.2+ with a valid SSL certificate (enterprise CA or public CA). Disable HTTP redirect to HTTPS. Disable weak cipher suites (RC4, DES, 3DES). SIEM contains all your security logs—unauthorized access would expose your entire security posture. Configure in Admin > Security > SSL.

criteria.text: Two-factor authentication is enabled for all administrator accounts
criteria.recommendation: Enable 2FA for all EventLog Analyzer accounts, especially administrators. Support TOTP, Duo Security, or RADIUS 2FA. A compromised SIEM admin account can allow attackers to disable alerting, delete evidence, and gain visibility into your detection capabilities.

criteria.text: Role-based access control is configured with least-privilege access
criteria.recommendation: Define granular RBAC roles: SOC Analyst (search + view reports), Compliance Auditor (reports only), Security Engineer (config + search), Administrator (full access). Prevent analysts from modifying alert configurations or deleting logs. Navigate to Admin > User Management.

criteria.text: Automated backup of EventLog Analyzer database and configuration is scheduled
criteria.recommendation: Configure daily automated backups of EventLog Analyzer database and configuration files. Store backups on a separate server or storage system. Test restore procedures quarterly. Log retention data loss is both a compliance failure and a forensic investigation blocker.

criteria.text: Log collection failure alerting is configured (detect when source stops sending logs)
criteria.recommendation: Configure alerts for log collection failures: if a critical source stops sending logs for >15 minutes, an alert should fire. Dead log sources create security blind spots that attackers can exploit. Navigate to Admin > Log Collection Monitoring > Source Health Alerts. Test by temporarily stopping a non-critical source.
